title: Pigs in blankets
subtitle: Chipolata sausages wrapped in streaky bacon
excerpt: Small chipolata sausages wrapped tightly in thin-cut streaky bacon and roasted until the bacon is crisp and the sausages are dark and sticky. The indispensable accompaniment to a British Christmas dinner.
sourceNotes: Pigs in blankets, the British Christmas accompaniment of chipolata sausages wrapped in streaky bacon, became established in the twentieth century and is now considered an essential part of the British Christmas dinner. The dish is documented in British Christmas cooking guides from the mid-twentieth century onwards.

recipe.freezeNotes: Assemble and freeze uncooked. Cook from frozen at 200°C fan for 35 to 40 minutes.
recipe.batchNotes: Scales easily. Make double or triple batches for large gatherings.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Wrap and refrigerate up to 24 hours before cooking.

content.text: The quality of pigs in blankets depends almost entirely on the quality of the ingredients: a proper pork chipolata, thin-cut streaky bacon rather than back bacon, and enough heat to render the fat and caramelise the outside. Thin-cut streaky bacon wraps tightly and crisps properly; thick-cut remains flabby and soft.
A small amount of honey or maple syrup brushed over before the final minutes of cooking gives the bacon an extra lacquered quality. This is optional but worth doing.

items.prepNote: thin-cut rashers, halved to make 24 short strips

content.text: Preheat the oven to 200°C fan. Halve {{streaky-bacon}} rashers of streaky bacon across their width to make short strips. Wrap each {{chipolata-sausages}} chipolata sausage in one piece of bacon, starting at one end and rolling tightly so the seam is underneath. Arrange in a roasting tin with a little space between each one.
Roast for 20 minutes. If glazing, brush with {{honey}} of honey and return to the oven for 5 minutes until the bacon is crisp and caramelised. Serve immediately.

items.cause: Thick-cut bacon was used, or the tin was overcrowded
items.fix: Use thin-cut streaky bacon only. Ensure there is space between each piece so hot air can circulate and render the fat.

items.symptom: Sausages are pale and undercooked inside
items.fix: Continue roasting for a further 5 to 10 minutes. The sausages should be fully cooked through with no pink meat.

items.cause: Not wrapped tightly enough, or the seam is on top
items.fix: Wrap tightly and always place seam-side down in the tin. The weight of the sausage holds the seam closed.

content.text: Pigs in blankets are so firmly embedded in the British Christmas dinner that their absence is widely considered a serious omission. The name itself is interchangeable with the dish in the British context: it refers to chipolatas wrapped in streaky bacon and nothing else, unlike the American version which is a cocktail sausage wrapped in pastry. They appear consistently on Christmas dinner menus in British food media from the 1970s onwards and their position alongside the turkey, stuffing, and roast potatoes is now effectively canonical.
